data.title: Stock futures fall after 2 days of gains. US Final GDP: 4.4%, Core PCE: 2.8%, 10-yr Treasury bond: 4.23%, 2-yr bond: 3.60%.Indices down: Dow -0.075%, S&P 500 -0.058%, Nasdaq 100 -0.16%. COF down 3.31% after Q4 results. REVB stock up.
data.description: U.S. stock futures saw a slight decline after two days of gains, influenced by President Trump removing tariffs on the EU due to an expanded U.S. access agreement with Greenland. In economic news, the U.S. Final GDP for Q3 was 4.4%, above the estimated 4.3%, with the PCE price index at 2.8% year-over-year. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.23%, while the two-year bond was at 3.60%. The Fed is expected to keep interest rates unchanged in January. Stock indices showed slight declines, with Dow Jones at -0.075%, S&P 500 at -0.058%, Nasdaq 100 at -0.16%, and Russell 2000 at -0.03%. ETFs like SPDR S&P 500 and Invesco QQQ Trust were lower in premarket trading. Capital One Financial saw shares decline by 3.31% after Q4 results, while Revelation Biosciences Inc. experienced an uptick. (Characters: 848)

data.title: Wonderinterest analyzed ESG investments in 2026; investing in leaders more selective; providing insights on global trends.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, in Limassol, Cyprus, there is a noticeable shift towards selective investments in ESG-focused leaders. Wonderinterest Trading Ltd. is offering insights into the trends of ESG-based investments in global capital markets for the year. They have identified key sustainability leaders for 2026.

data.title: Stock futures dip after 2 days of gains. US Final GDP at 4.4%, PCE price at 2.8%. Treasury bonds at 4.23% and 3.60%. Dow Jones -0.075%, S&P 500 -0.058%, Nasdaq 100 -0.16%. Capital One Financial down 3.31%, Revelation Biosciences up. Fed likely to maintain rates in January.
data.description: On Friday, U.S. stock futures dipped slightly following two days of gains in major benchmark indices, partly due to President Trump withdrawing tariffs against the EU and securing expanded U.S. access to Greenland. The Final GDP for the third quarter exceeded estimates at 4.4%, with the PCE price index and Core PCE both at 2.8%. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.23%, and the two-year bond was at 3.60%. Fed projections suggest a 95% chance of interest rates staying the same in January. Index performances showed slight declines: Dow Jones -0.075%, S&P 500 -0.058%, Nasdaq 100 -0.16%, Russell 2000 -0.03%. ETFs SPY and QQQ tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 were down in premarket, with SPY at $688.44 (-0.078%) and QQQ at $619.49 (-0.20%). Capital One Financial (COF) shares dropped 3.31% pre-market due to Q4 earnings missing estimates. Revelation Biosciences (REVB) saw a rise in NASDAQ.

data.title: European enterprises are turning to Oracle cloud, utilizing AI-driven solutions; report from ISG reveals the trend.
data.description: In London, according to a report from ISG, European businesses are showing a growing preference for Oracle cloud platforms, particularly when paired with partners offering AI-based solutions. The report highlights a significant rise in adoption rates, emphasizing the trend towards leveraging cloud technology. The partnership with AI-driven solution providers has proven to be particularly popular among enterprises. This shift towards Oracle cloud environments is indicative of a broader movement within the European business sector, with numbers pointing towards a clear trend.

data.title: LEPAS introduces new energy vehicle brand on LEX Platform by Chery Group, aiming for global market domination.
data.description: In WUHU, China, a new energy vehicle brand named LEPAS has been introduced on Jan. 23, 2026. This brand is specifically designed for global markets and is established on the advanced LEX Platform. Created as a part of the Chery Group's global technology ecosystem, LEX represents a significant advancement setting a new standard for future generations of vehicles.
